prompt: Art Nouveau botanical illustration plate, 1890s scientific natural history style in the manner of Ernst Haeckel's Kunstformen der Natur. A museum-quality specimen plate titled 'Sylva Stochastica / Random Forest Classifier' depicting five distinct decision tree specimens as botanical plants, each rendered as a unique branching specimen with sinuous whiplash Art Nouveau curves. Each tree-plant has: a clearly labeled ROOT NODE at the soil, branching stems as DECISION NODES with handwritten Gini-impurity values (0.0–0.5) in italic script, forking branches labeled with data feature names (petal length, sepal width) in flowing copperplate lettering, and terminal LEAF NODES in three distinct colors (sage green, deep burgundy, dusty ochre) representing three classification outcomes. Visual grammar: flat unmodulated color fills separated by fine ink outline, no photographic shading, ivory aged paper background, sinuous organic stems flowing like Mucha tendrils, decorative symmetrical botanical border of intertwined vines framing the entire plate, small handwritten specimen numbers I through V beneath each tree, ensemble voting arrows shown as delicate stylized bees cross-pollinating between specimens, final prediction shown as a blooming flower crown. Color palette strictly: aged ivory #F5F0DC background, malachite green #2D6A4F, deep burgundy #6B1F2A, amber ochre #C9932B, charcoal ink #1A1A1A outlines, dusty rose #C9847A accent. Chromolithography print quality. Negative: no photography, no gradients, no modern fonts, no digital look, no 3D rendering, no photorealism, no white background, no cartoon style.
concept: Random Forest Classifier — an ensemble of decision trees where each tree votes on a classification and the majority prediction wins
tradition_approach: Sinuous whiplash curves for decision branches, flat chromolithography color fills, ivory aged paper, Haeckel-style specimen plate layout with copperplate Latin labels, decorative vine border integrating concept elements.
